payload.description: Fixture future earnings event for lifecycle testing.

positioning.public_description: Parallax is a research accountability platform for trading ideas. It turns a thesis into an evidence-linked dossier, challenges assumptions, surfaces invalidators, and monitors whether the thesis remains defensible.
positioning.legal_posture: Research support only. Parallax is not a broker, investment adviser, signal service, tax adviser, legal adviser, or live execution system.
positioning.product_rule: The general product ceiling is no_trade, research_needed, watchlist, or paper_trade_candidate. Live execution is excluded from the general product.
positioning.user_promise: Make unsupported conviction hard to hide.

disclosures: Parallax output is not investment advice or a recommendation to buy, sell, hold, or short any security.
The dossier is a research artifact; it can be wrong, stale, incomplete, or based on flawed data.
Markets change continuously, so every thesis must have freshness, invalidation, and recheck rules.
Generated text cannot be the source of financial calculations; numeric claims must trace back to tool outputs.
Paper-trading output is simulation only and must not be marketed as proof of future returns.
Live execution is not part of the general Parallax product.
prohibited_claims: Parallax guarantees profit or avoids loss.
Parallax tells users what to buy or sell.
Parallax replaces a financial adviser, broker, risk manager, or compliance reviewer.
Parallax can safely execute live trades without explicit human and regulated partner controls.
Paper-trading or backtest outcomes prove future live performance.

controls.message: Request is inside the research-support product boundary.

upcoming_events.description: Fixture future earnings event for lifecycle testing.

claim_packets.thesis: NVDA does not show an extreme volatility regime in the current snapshot.
assumptions: Regime proxy uses realized volatility and dependency correlation.
risks: Regime classifier is deliberately simple in v0.

claim_packets.thesis: The 20-period momentum is positive, but the edge remains conditional on costs, volatility, and confirmation.
assumptions: Momentum proxy is a first-pass signal, not a validated strategy.
base_rates: Backtest/walk-forward module is required before paper escalation.

claim_packets.thesis: No high-materiality event is present in the fixture snapshot; fundamental evidence is limited in v0.
assumptions: Fundamental data is adapter-provided and must carry source, freshness, and license metadata.
invalidators: New filing, guidance, or earnings event changes the premise.
required_checks: Add filings/earnings adapter before stronger fundamental claims.

claim_packets.thesis: Technical conditions are constructive, with positive latest return and acceptable liquidity proxy.
assumptions: Technical analysis uses simple price/volume proxies in v0.

claim_packets.thesis: Cross-asset context is approximated through dependency correlations; macro evidence is not yet rich enough for strong claims.
assumptions: Dependency correlation is a proxy for cross-asset sensitivity.
invalidators: Sector ETF or benchmark breaks correlation regime.
required_checks: Rates, FX, and macro calendar adapters.

claim_packets.thesis: News and sentiment are not deeply integrated in v0, so no strong sentiment claim is made.
assumptions: Event calendar is a proxy until news ingestion is connected.

claim_packets.thesis: Evidence snapshot passes v0 freshness checks.
invalidators: Any central data item becomes stale, missing, restricted, or conflicting.

claim_packets.thesis: Portfolio risk allows watchlist-level consideration, subject to sizing and freshness.
assumptions: Risk limits come from fixture portfolio profile.

claim_packets.thesis: Execution proxy is acceptable for watchlist or paper-trade analysis.
assumptions: Execution model is a range-based proxy until venue/order-book data exists.

claim_packets.thesis: No fixture restricted-list conflict is present.
assumptions: Fixture restricted list represents the active mandate for this prototype.

claim_packets.thesis: The v0 deterministic rule council is registered as a prototype model and is replayable from audit bundles.
assumptions: This is a prototype validator; production requires a model registry and validation report.
invalidators: Unregistered model or prompt configuration is used.
required_checks: Governance phase validation before production deployment.

claim_packets.thesis: The strongest countercase is that this thesis is chasing already-visible price action without enough validated edge.
assumptions: Red-team role intentionally stresses downside and overconfidence.
required_checks: Define confirmation and kill conditions before action.
Add filings/earnings adapter before stronger fundamental claims.

markdown_documents.markdown:
# NVDA swing thesis dossier

Generated: 2026-05-01T14:30:00Z

## Decision

- Action class: paper_trade_candidate
- Thesis state: active
- Confidence: 0.63
- Freshness: 1
- Next trigger: Volatility regime changes materially.

## Thesis

phase seven regulated partner execution candidate

## Product Boundary

- Status: allowed
- Effective action ceiling: paper_trade_candidate
- Legal posture: Research support only. Parallax is not a broker, investment adviser, signal service, tax adviser, legal adviser, or live execution system.

## Council Summary

8 support, 0 oppose, 2 need more data.

## Council Evaluation

- Provider: deterministic_rule_council_v0
- Passed: true
- Problems: 0
- Warnings: 0
- Contexts: 0
- Estimated model cost: n/a

## Strongest Bull Case

The 20-period momentum is positive, but the edge remains conditional on costs, volatility, and confirmation.

## Strongest Bear Case

The strongest countercase is that this thesis is chasing already-visible price action without enough validated edge.

## Dissent

None recorded.

## Vetoes

None.

## Invalidators

- Volatility regime changes materially.
- Momentum turns negative.
- Volatility expands beyond threshold.
- New filing, guidance, or earnings event changes the premise.
- Price loses the trigger level.
- Liquidity deteriorates.
- Cost estimate widens.
- Sector ETF or benchmark breaks correlation regime.
- Material news arrives.
- Source reliability changes.
- Any central data item becomes stale, missing, restricted, or conflicting.
- Exposure increases.
- Drawdown or volatility breaches threshold.
- Spread widens.
- Relative volume collapses.
- Slippage estimate rises.
- Corporate action changes price comparability.
- Restricted list changes.
- Mandate constraints change.
- Unregistered model or prompt configuration is used.
- Replay fails.
- Confirmation fails.
- Event risk changes premise.
- Portfolio exposure worsens.

## Lifecycle Triggers

- invalidate/price: `last_price < 111.73` - Price moved below the thesis invalidation band.
- recheck/price: `last_price > 117.47` - Price moved enough to require a new risk/reward check.
- downgrade/volatility: `annualized_volatility_20 > 0.850` - Volatility expanded beyond the allowed thesis envelope.
- downgrade/time: `now > 2026-05-02T14:30:00.000Z` - Evidence snapshot expired for this horizon.

## Audit

- Dossier ID: dos_54f0333b49c3
- Evidence snapshot: snap_55562912b767
- Tool outputs: 11
- Claim packets: 12

workspace.name: Phase Seven Execution Desk
workspace.product_boundary: Research, watchlist, and paper-trade governance only; no live execution.

review_policy.release_rule: A dossier is team-release-ready only when registry validation passes, required approvals are present, and no review has requested changes or rejected the thesis.

soc2_readiness_program.name: Control Environment
soc2_readiness_program.description: Workspace owner and active role directory are configured.

soc2_readiness_program.name: Communication And Information
soc2_readiness_program.description: Review assignments, comments, and approvals are persisted as audit records.

soc2_readiness_program.name: Risk Assessment
soc2_readiness_program.description: Release readiness checks combine dossier validation with required human approvals.

soc2_readiness_program.name: Logical Access
soc2_readiness_program.description: Approval authority is role-bound by review type.

soc2_readiness_program.name: Change Management
soc2_readiness_program.description: Model/tool registry and validation status are included in release controls.

soc2_readiness_program.name: Availability And Audit Export
soc2_readiness_program.description: A portable governance export can reconstruct team review state.

approvals.rationale: Thesis is evidence-linked and dissent is preserved.
Risk controls allow only small sandbox handoff.
Partner path remains controlled and non-broker.
Model and tool registry validation passes.

partner-execution.json.product_boundary: Partner execution is disabled by default. Parallax can create auditable partner handoff records only after legal, compliance, market-access, team-release, human-approval, pre-trade, and kill-switch controls pass.

regulatory_obligations.control: Production routing requires a registered/regulated execution partner; Parallax is not a broker.
Partner handoff requires documented financial and regulatory risk controls before any market-access route.
Review assignments, approvals, and supervisory decisions must be reconstructable.
Tickets, approvals, submissions, and post-trade reviews are retained in the local execution ledger.

partners.name: Regulated Partner Sandbox
partners.product_boundary: Parallax does not custody assets, provide broker services, or bypass partner controls.

legal_approvals.memo: Sandbox handoff approved for governed paper candidates only.
Production paperwork exists, but adapter remains locked.
